title: Snowflake’s product revenue outlook falls short of investor ambition, shares fall
content:
By Kritika Lamba

Dec 3 (Reuters) - Snowflake forecast fourth-quarter product revenue above analysts’ estimates but falls short of lofty investor expectations for stronger growth, sending its shares down about 8% in extended trading.

The cloud data analytics firm, whose shares have surged more than 70% this year, expects product revenue of $1.19 billion to $1.20 billion for the fourth quarter, representing 27% growth. The forecast was above analysts’ estimates of $1.18 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

"Given the dramatic appreciation in share price this year, investors were expecting guidance of more than 30%. We believe this growth will materialize next quarter allowing shares to continue appreciating," said D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria.

Snowflake expanded its partnerships to strengthen its agentic AI and cloud ecosystem, announcing a multi-year $200 million agreement with Anthropic to bring Claude models to its platform for advanced reasoning and secure multi-step analysis.

The company also deepened ties with Accenture to help enterprises scale generative AI innovation, and unveiled new integrations with Amazon Web Services after surpassing $2 billion in sales on the AWS Marketplace this year.

The Bozeman, Montana-based company is similarly working with Google’s Gemini AI and other partners to embed large language models into its tools so customers can build and run AI applications directly on Snowflake-managed data.

The push comes as businesses accelerate generative AI strategies, using large language models for analytics, automation and customer engagement thereby fueling demand for AI-enabled cloud services across sectors, benefiting companies like Snowflake.

The company reported quarterly revenue of $1.21 billion, beating analysts’ estimates of $1.18 billion.

title: C3.ai shares tick higher after narrower loss, strong federal bookings in Q2
content:
Investing.com -- C3.ai posted a narrower fiscal second-quarter loss and slightly topped revenue expectations, helped by a sharp pickup in federal-sector demand and stronger partner-driven deal activity.

The company reported an adjusted loss of $0.25 per share, beating analysts’ expectations of a $0.33 loss. Revenue came in at $75.1 million, just above the $74.93 million consensus, and up 7% sequentially.

Shares rose about 1.2% in extended trading.

CEO Stephen Ehikian said growth was underpinned by “excellent performance” in federal accounts and rising large-deal activity, calling the federal market a “large growth vector.”

Federal bookings jumped 89% year over year, even with disruption from the government shutdown, and accounted for 45% of total bookings. The company signed new or expanded agreements with agencies including the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, the U.S. Army, Naval Air Warfare Center, and the U.S. Intelligence Community.

Overall bookings rose 49% quarter-on-quarter, with 17 deals over $1 million and six above $5 million. About 89% of all bookings flowed through C3.ai’s partner ecosystem, including Microsoft, AWS, and Booz Allen Hamilton. The joint Microsoft pipeline rose 146% year over year, while the AWS pipeline increased 172%.

Subscription revenue, which makes up the bulk of the business, was $70.2 million, or 93% of total revenue. GAAP gross margin was 40%, and non-GAAP gross margin reached 54%. The company ended the quarter with $675 million in cash and marketable securities.

For fiscal 2026, C3.ai guided revenue to $289.5–309.5 million, bracketing the $299.5 million analyst consensus. For the current quarter, it expects $72–80 million in revenue, compared with expectations of $75.6 million.

Ehikian said the company has set “precise operational objectives” across business units and sees a “clear pathway” toward a return to growth, cash generation and non-GAAP profitability.

title: Amazon adds news tab to Prime Video in user expansion push
content:
Dec 3 (Reuters) - Amazon.com said on Wednesday it is rolling out a dedicated news tab on its Prime Video streaming service, which will become available to all U.S. customers for free by the end of the year.

The section, hosting news networks that include ABC News Live, CBS News 24/7, LiveNOW from Fox, CNN Headlines and NBC News NOW, will cover a broad selection of topics such as world, national and regional news.

The e-commerce giant is looking to make it more convenient for customers to access live networks and grow its user base as it grapples with strong competition from rivals looking to increase market share.

Separately on Wednesday, Amazon also announced a subscription channel where customers can watch a selection of Sony Pictures movies and shows.

Sony Pictures Core is available on Prime Video to customers in the U.S. and Canada for $6.99 per month.

title: Amazon unveils advanced AI model customization tools for developers
content:
The new features include Reinforcement Fine Tuning (RFT) in Amazon Bedrock and serverless model customization with reinforcement learning in Amazon SageMaker AI. These tools aim to simplify the process of tailoring AI models to specific use cases while improving accuracy.

According to AWS, the Amazon SageMaker AI enhancements can reduce advanced model customization workflows from months to days, potentially accelerating AI development cycles and helping organizations bring new solutions to market faster.

The announcement comes as cloud providers compete to offer more accessible AI development tools that can be used by a wider range of developers beyond AI specialists.

In other recent news, Amazon has garnered attention with several analyst firms reiterating their positive ratings following announcements at the AWS re:Invent conference. Piper Sandler reaffirmed its Overweight rating, highlighting positive holiday shopping data and developments in Amazon Web Services (AWS). The firm referenced Adobe data indicating a projected 5% increase in holiday season spending for 2025. Stifel maintained its Buy rating and a $295 price target after AWS CEO Matt Garman’s keynote presentation. Cantor Fitzgerald also reiterated an Overweight rating with a $315 price target, emphasizing the release of Trn3 UltraServers and the development of Trn4, which boasts improved performance. Citizens echoed this sentiment with a Market Outperform rating and a $300 price target, citing increased confidence in Amazon’s competitive position in artificial intelligence. These developments reflect a strong focus on AWS innovations and positive market expectations.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: AWS announces new AI model customization features
content:
Amazon Web Services announced new artificial intelligence model customization capabilities at its re:Invent conference in Las Vegas. The company introduced Reinforcement Fine Tuning in Amazon Bedrock and serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI with reinforcement learning.

According to the announcement, Reinforcement Fine Tuning in Amazon Bedrock aims to make it easier for developers to tailor models to specific use cases and improve accuracy. The feature allows customers to customize foundation models without requiring extensive machine learning expertise.

The new Amazon SageMaker AI capabilities include serverless model customization workflows that the company states can reduce development time from months to days. The service incorporates reinforcement learning techniques to help developers build AI agents more efficiently.

AWS (AMZN) positioned these updates as part of its effort to make advanced model customization accessible to developers regardless of their technical background. The features are designed to reduce costs while improving performance for customers building AI applications.

The announcements were made as part of AWS re:Invent, the company’s annual cloud computing conference. Both Amazon Bedrock and Amazon SageMaker AI are existing AWS services that provide machine learning and artificial intelligence capabilities to enterprise customers.

title: Dynatrace achieves AWS agentic AI specialization, wins partner award
content:
Dynatrace (DT) announced at AWS re:Invent 2025 that it achieved AWS Agentic AI Specialization and was named AWS Public Sector Technology Partner of the Year for Latin America.

The AWS Agentic AI Specialization is a new category within the AWS AI Competency program. To earn this designation, Dynatrace demonstrated technical expertise and customer success in monitoring and governing agentic AI systems in production environments.

The company received the Latin America Public Sector Technology Partner of the Year award, recognizing its solutions supporting government, education, and nonprofit organizations across the region. This follows Dynatrace’s 2024 recognition as EMEA Technology Partner of the Year.

Dynatrace also announced expanded AWS service integrations, including a Cloud Operations Solution for automatic discovery of AWS services, integration with AWS DevOps Agent for faster root cause identification, and observability capabilities for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore.

The company introduced its Kiro autonomous agent to accelerate developer productivity by handling tasks from bug triage to feature implementation. Additional integrations include AWS Security Hub for cloud security posture monitoring.

"Our collaboration with AWS continues to drive innovation and deliver meaningful outcomes for our customers," said Jay Snyder, senior vice president of global partner and alliances at Dynatrace.

The integrations are designed to help enterprises reduce mean time to resolution, prevent outages through automated remediation, and strengthen security across cloud environments, according to the company’s statement.

title: NVIDIA’s architecture delivers 10x performance leap for complex AI models
content:
Investing.com -- NVIDIA’s GB200 NVL72 rack-scale system is delivering a 10x performance leap for mixture-of-experts (MoE) AI models compared to previous generation hardware, transforming how frontier AI models are deployed, according to a latest post on the company’s blog.

MoE architecture, which mimics the human brain by activating only relevant "expert" components for each task, has become the dominant approach for top AI models. The Artificial Analysis leaderboard shows the top 10 most intelligent open-source models all use MoE architecture, including DeepSeek-R1, Moonshot AI’s Kimi K2 Thinking, OpenAI’s gpt-oss-120B, and Mistral Large 3.

The NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 system combines 72 Blackwell GPUs working as a single unit, delivering 1.4 exaflops of AI performance with 30TB of shared memory. The system’s NVLink Switch creates a unified fabric with 130 TB/s of connectivity, allowing experts to be distributed across up to 72 GPUs.

This design directly addresses key MoE scaling challenges by reducing the number of experts per GPU and accelerating communication between experts. The system also incorporates the NVIDIA Dynamo framework and NVFP4 format to further enhance performance.

"At CoreWeave, our customers are leveraging our platform to put mixture-of-experts models into production as they build agentic workflows," said Peter Salanki, cofounder and chief technology officer at CoreWeave.

The GB200 NVL72 is being deployed by major cloud providers including Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, among others.

DeepL is using the Blackwell NVL72 system to train its next-generation AI models. "DeepL is leveraging NVIDIA GB200 hardware to train mixture-of-experts models, advancing its model architecture to improve efficiency during training and inference," said Paul Busch, research team lead at DeepL.

The 10x performance improvement translates to 10x token revenue, significantly improving the economics of AI deployment in power-constrained data centers. NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang highlighted this performance leap for DeepSeek-R1 at NVIDIA GTC Washington, D.C.

Fireworks AI has deployed Kimi K2 on the NVIDIA B200 platform. "NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack-scale design makes MoE model serving dramatically more efficient," said Lin Qiao, cofounder and CEO of Fireworks AI.

The new Kinnie model also achieved a 10x performance gain on the GB200 NVL72 compared to the H200, resulting in better user experience, lower per-token cost, and higher energy efficiency.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Astera Labs stock rebounds as Mizuho sees Amazon-Nvidia deal as positive
content:
The steep decline was described as "an over-reaction on fast money sellers and shorts" by Mizuho’s Klein, who noted that several sell-side analysts have defended the stock, stating the Nvidia deal is actually additive with higher dollar content potential for Astera Labs in Amazon’s new Trainium 4 ASIC currently in design for 2027.

Initial market concerns centered on whether the new partnership would reduce space and dollar content for Astera’s interconnect technologies, which are UALink and PCIe based, as Amazon opens its Trainium ASIC and stock to Nvidia’s high-speed interconnect platform.

Astera Labs management has reportedly addressed investor concerns, clarifying that UALink would remain a core option for Trainium 4, and while NVLink would be offered, Astera products could still be used in these designs without being blocked out.

In other recent news, Credo Technology Group has seen a series of positive developments. The company reported strong earnings for the October quarter, with revenue reaching $268 million and earnings per share of $0.67, surpassing consensus estimates. Credo also provided guidance for the January quarter, projecting revenue of $340 million, which is approximately 38% above the consensus estimate. Analysts have responded favorably to these results, with several firms, including TD Cowen, Roth/MKM, Needham, Mizuho, and Stifel, raising their price targets on Credo Technology. TD Cowen increased its target to $240, citing strong results and guidance, while Roth/MKM raised its target to $250, highlighting a broadening customer base. Needham noted the company’s Active Electrical Cable strength, raising its target to $220 and increasing its fiscal 2027 revenue estimate by $350 million. Mizuho and Stifel both set their price targets at $225, recognizing the company’s earnings beat and revenue growth. These developments underscore the positive momentum for Credo Technology in the market.

title: Nebius leverages Microsoft, Meta contracts for AI expansion
content:
By Toby Sterling and Lucy Raitano

AMSTERDAM/LONDON, Dec 3 (Reuters) - Netherlands-based data centre operator Nebius will leverage recent multi-billion dollar contracts with Microsoft and Meta to build its business with traditional firms and emerging AI companies that could be future tech titans, its co-founder said.

A $17 billion deal with Microsoft in September and another $3 billion tie-up with Meta have helped propel the company’s Nasdaq-listed stock 248% higher this year, pushing its market capitalisation past $25 billion.

The firm is now widening its client base - especially given fears an AI bubble is already underway.

"We are very bullish," Roman Chernin told Reuters in an interview, adding that ebbs are natural during a technological revolution but the market for supplying companies with access to AI infrastructure could grow ten- or even a hundred-fold, as firms are only beginning to use AI models that are themselves undergoing rapid advances.

As a smaller player in the booming sector, the firm is preparing for harder times by developing high-margin services and long-term relationships.

"We should be ready (for when) the winter will come," Chernin said, adding Nebius would then act as a consolidator.

EUROPE’S BIGGEST NEOCLOUD FIRM

Legacy enterprises are just beginning to tap Nebius’ services, spanning sectors like manufacturing, retail and banking  - with pharmaceutical firms and financial institutions like hedge funds moving faster, Chernin said.

Nebius, which has a partnership with Nvidia, is Europe’s biggest ’neocloud’ firm, offering access to the powerful but expensive graphics processing units (GPUs) needed for AI.

It also offers the software needed to use them effectively and to run specialized AI applications, putting it in competition with both traditional data centre operators and U.S. "hyperscalers" like Amazon and Google.

Nebius’ top market by revenue and installed capacity is the United States, but it also has data centres in Europe, including in Britain, Iceland, Finland and France. It plans to secure 2.5 gigawatts of contracted power for facilities across both regions by the end of 2026, reflecting customer demand.

Capacity sold to European "neocloud" providers rose 211% to 414 megawatts in the first nine months of 2025 from a year earlier, according to real estate services firm CBRE.

MARGIN NOT REVENUE

Chernin said Nebius prioritised margins, not revenue, when negotiating hyperscaler deals.

"We are thinking about it as a system, so we signed those (large) deals having in mind that we need to finance the rest," he said.

Following the Microsoft deal, Nebius raised $4.2 billion through a public share offering and sale of convertible notes.

Nebius’ early client base included AI natives like French model-maker Mistral, U.S. coding specialist Cursor, and German visual AI firm Black Forest - companies poised to become a new generation of large firms, Chernin said.

The company’s growth in 2026 will come from existing digital firms that are early AI adopters, such as customers Shopify, Prosus, and ServiceNow, that have always relied on U.S. cloud services but see advantages in Nebius’s specialised AI offerings, validating the company’s model, he added.

Beyond 2027, the company hopes to supply services to mainstream companies, which Chernin said would do more for European sovereignty than attracting investments in large data centres by U.S. firms.

"Where will Siemens and BMW go when they will need to adopt AI on a large scale?" he said.

"The North Star ambition of Nebius is to exist and be relevant for that market."

title: Marvell shares soar as chipmaker boosts its AI drive with Celestial deal
content:
Dec 3 (Reuters) - Marvell Technology jumped 9.3% to a nine-month high on Wednesday after its $3.25 billion deal to acquire semiconductor startup Celestial AI bolstered investor confidence in the chipmaker’s push into next-gen AI infrastructure.

The company is doubling down on AI infrastructure to regain momentum after a tough year marked by intense competition in its custom AI chips and networking. Its shares are down about 16% so far this year, through Tuesday’s close.

If Wednesday’s gains hold, the rally would add more than $6.3 billion to Marvell’s market value.

The Celestial AI deal adds photonic tech for next-gen data centers, putting Marvell in direct competition with Broadcom and Nvidia as hyperscalers race to build faster, energy-efficient systems amid the generative AI boom.

The company also issued a warrant to Amazon.com, allowing the tech giant to buy Marvell shares tied to its purchases of photonic fabric products through 2030.

"In our view, Celestial AI, with blessing from Amazon, bolsters Marvell’s CPO (co-packaged optics) technology for custom XPUs and scale-up switching, making Marvell a stronger contender," TD Cowen analysts said.

Photonics fabric tech uses light instead of electrical signals to link AI chips and memory for next-gen data centers.

The Celestial tech will be used in Marvell’s next-generation photonics-related infrastructure products, which will contribute to a new $10 billion market for Marvell, CEO Matt Murphy told Reuters on Tuesday.

Celestial’s technology is projected to contribute $500 million in annualized revenue by late-fiscal 2028, doubling to $1 billion a year later.

Marvell expects roughly $10 billion in revenue for its next fiscal year, fueled by a 25% jump in data center sales. The Santa Clara, California-based company estimated its custom chip revenue to rise 20% next year.

Marvell trades at a 12-month forward price-to-earnings ratio of 27.25, below Broadcom’s 38.39, according to LSEG data.

title: Benchmark reiterates Buy rating on Shopify stock after BFCM sales growth
content:
Shopify reported BFCM weekend sales grew 27% year-over-year (24% on a foreign exchange neutral basis) to $14.6 billion, representing 12.1% of fourth-quarter estimated gross merchandise value (GMV). This growth aligns with the company’s impressive 30.25% overall revenue growth over the last twelve months.

This BFCM performance is approximately 20 basis points below Shopify’s 2022-2024 average of 12.3%, but Benchmark believes it suggests fourth-quarter GMV could reach $118.9 billion compared to consensus estimates of $121.0 billion.

Benchmark noted the slight decline in BFCM’s percentage of quarterly GMV likely reflects increasing fourth-quarter competitive discounting and sales occurring outside the BFCM weekend, such as Amazon’s Prime Days on October 7-8.

The research firm concluded that considering the continuation of this trend, Shopify’s BFCM performance actually suggests support for fourth-quarter GMV at or above consensus estimates, potentially reaching up to $121.7 billion.

In other recent news, Shopify reported record sales of $14.6 billion during the Black Friday-Cyber Monday period, marking a 27% increase from the previous year. The company noted that sales grew 24% on a constant currency basis, with peak transactions reaching $5.1 million per minute on Black Friday. Despite these impressive figures, the growth fell slightly short of the fourth-quarter consensus Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) estimate of approximately 28%. Analysts from Mizuho have maintained a Neutral rating on Shopify, with a price target of $150.00, while Citizens kept its Market Outperform rating with a $185.00 price target. Raymond James also maintained its Market Perform rating, acknowledging Shopify’s strong sales growth of 25% year-over-year, outperforming competitors like Salesforce and Adobe. However, Oppenheimer pointed out that Shopify’s Black Friday sales of $6.2 billion may face scrutiny as they lag behind the expected GMV growth. Overall, these developments highlight Shopify’s robust performance during the critical holiday shopping period.

title: Astera Labs stock rises after analysts defend NVLink strategy
content:
Investing.com -- Astera Labs (NASDAQ:ALAB) stock rose 7% in pre-market trading Wednesday, partially recovering from Tuesday’s 13.5% decline after analysts defended the company’s position in the NVLink ecosystem.

The stock tumbled Tuesday following AWS re:Invent announcements about Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) partnering with NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) to integrate NVLink Fusion into its Trainium 4 XPU deployments, which sparked investor concerns about Astera’s market position.

Stifel analyst Tore Svanberg maintained a Buy rating and $200 price target on Astera Labs, addressing market fears. "We believe these fears are misplaced and would be acquirers of ALAB stock on weakness. ALAB’s support for the NVLink ecosystem and more active role in hyperscaler custom designs mean the company should have a continued solid content opportunity in NVLink Fusion designs across hyperscaler deployments," Svanberg noted.

Similarly, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore reiterated an Overweight rating with a $210 price target, explaining that Tuesday’s announcement actually expands Astera’s addressable market. "The stock sold off 13% on Tuesday because this NVLink adoption was viewed as unfavorable, while in our view the announcement is clearly an incremental positive," Moore commented.

Astera Labs announced yesterday it was building on its initial NVLink ecosystem support by collaborating with hyperscalers to support NVLink connectivity in complex deployments across XPUs and GPUs, taking a more active role in the ecosystem.

title: Anthropic plans an IPO as early as 2026, FT reports
content:
Dec 2 (Reuters) - Anthropic, the artificial intelligence startup backed by Alphabet’s Google and Amazon.com, has hired the law firm Wilson Sonsini to prepare for an initial public offering that could take place as early as 2026, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday.

An IPO would give the company, which operates the AI chatbot Claude, a more efficient way to raise capital and provide leverage for bigger acquisitions through public stock. The move comes as AI adoption gains pace, driven by higher enterprise tech spending and growing investor appetite.

An Anthropic spokesperson told Reuters the company has not decided when or even if it will go public.

The company has previously turned to Wilson Sonsini for a range of corporate matters. The law firm did not immediately respond to Reuters’ request for comment on the FT report outside normal business hours.

Anthropic could be prepared to list in 2026, according to the report. The startup has also discussed with major investment banks about a potential IPO, the newspaper said, citing sources.

The FT report said, however, that the talks are in early stages and informal, indicating the company is still far from selecting its IPO underwriters.

Anthropic is negotiating a private funding round that could give the startup a valuation exceeding $300 billion, the report added.

Dario Amodei-led Anthropic is projecting to more than double and potentially nearly triple its annualized revenue run rate to around $26 billion next year. It has more than 300,000 business and enterprise customers.

Microsoft-backed OpenAI, a major rival, is preparing for what could rank among the largest IPO ever, with a potential valuation of up to $1 trillion. The firm is laying the groundwork to go public and may file with securities regulators as early as the second half of 2026, Reuters has reported.

OpenAI’s chief financial officer, Sarah Friar, said later in November that a listing is not in the startup’s near-term plans.

Last month, Microsoft and Nvidia announced plans to invest up to $15 billion in Anthropic as the AI firm made a $30 billion commitment to use Microsoft’s cloud services.

Founded in 2021 by former OpenAI staff, Anthropic was recently valued at $183 billion.

title: Analysis-Strong start to online holiday shopping masks signs of a fragile U.S. consumer
content:
By Nicholas P. Brown

NEW YORK, Dec 2 (Reuters) - The U.S. holiday shopping season kicked off to record online sales. But underlying signs of economic fragility signal a pullback in spending could be looming.

Consumer confidence fell again in November. Still, Americans spent a record $44.2 billion online during the period retailers call Cyber Week - the U.S. shopping bonanza that runs from Thanksgiving through the following Monday - according to Adobe Analytics, which tracks shopper visits to online retail websites.

Comparable sales figures for brick-and-mortar stores were not yet available.

Record top-line sales should be taken "with a pinch of salt," said John Mercer, head of global research at consumer analytics firm Coresight. Costs have risen, in part due to tariffs, and wealthy shoppers "have been holding up retail all year," Mercer said.

Dig deeper, and signs of trepidatious consumers abounded over Cyber Week. Shoppers at Target and Walmart cut down on impulse purchases. More consumers than ever were tapping short-term debt from buy-now-pay-later providers, Adobe data showed. Amazon.com and other retailers extended discounts to everyday items like batteries and cleaning supplies, suggesting consumers were hunting for deals just to afford household staples, said Jack O’Leary, director of e-commerce insights at consumer intelligence firm NielsenIQ.

Surveys by market research firm Kantar show that, in the last month, the percentage of consumers who feel able to afford everyday essentials has dropped four percentage points, though the figure remains well above 2023 lows.

In a November survey by consumer analytics firm CivicScience, more than two-thirds of likely shoppers said the price impact of tariffs would affect their shopping, either spurring them to buy gifts earlier, or reducing their hauls.

Discounts were steep this year, suggesting consumers needed more motivation than usual to spend. "Sentiment reflects anxiety, behavior reflects capacity, and discounting bridges the gap," said Kent Smetters, an economics professor at the University of Pennsylvania’s Wharton School.

POTENTIAL PULLBACK

Those behaviors may not signal a recession, but they illustrate "a slow erosion" of financial health for families, said Julie Craig, Kantar’s vice president of shopper insights. Craig pointed to Kantar surveys showing a year-to-year decrease in the share of shoppers who say they have enough savings to weather an emergency.

In November, RBC analysts reported lower- and middle-income consumers were "running out of steam," citing flat retail spending that had followed months of front-loading ahead of tariff costs.

The impact of that exhaustion is more likely to emerge in the months after the holiday shopping season, experts say. The new year will bring more direct impacts from stressors like tariff costs, the recent government shutdown, and the related pause in SNAP benefits. "It paints a picture of an increasingly anxious shopper who may be spending in bursts, then pulling back to compensate," Craig said.

Job cuts, which could affect spending, are less common just ahead of the holidays but more common after, said Marshal Cohen, chief retail advisor at consumer research firm Circana.

Mark Mathews, chief economist of the National Retail Federation trade group, is more optimistic. Consumer sentiment has been out of sync with actual spending for about five years, and major contractions have been rare, he said.

"Consumers are trying to stretch their dollar, trading down to lower-priced stores, trying to assuage low sentiment," he said. "But what they’re not doing is not spending."

title: Trading Day: Tech, Fed hopes do the heavy lifting
content:
By Jamie McGeever

ORLANDO, Florida, Dec 2 (Reuters) - World stocks rose on Tuesday, regaining their poise after wobbling the previous day and lifted by an extended upswing in tech and AI sentiment as investors also continued to wager the Federal Reserve will lower U.S. interest rates next week.

More on that below. In my column today, I look at how overseas demand for U.S. equities has re-accelerated in recent months and is running at its fastest pace on record. The big question, of course, is can this continue into next year?

If you have more time to read, here are a few articles I recommend to help you make sense of what happened in markets today. 1. Trump says he will announce Fed chief nominee in early2026 2. Strong start to online holiday shopping masks signs of afragile U.S. consumer 3. EXCLUSIVE-China issues first batch of streamlined rareearth export licences, source says 4. SPECIAL REPORT-China floods the world with gasoline carsit can’t sell at home 5. 2026 market calls already laced with ’Buy, but...’: MikeDolan

Today’s Key Market Moves * STOCKS: Wall Street in the green: S&P 500 +0.2%, Nasdaq+0.6%. Germany +0.5%, South Korea +2%, Japan’s TOPIX +2.7%,China in the red. * SECTORS/SHARES: Boeing +10%, Intel +8.5%. Surprisingly,eight U.S. sectors fall and only three rise. Tech andindustrials +0.9%, energy -1.3%. * FX: Indian rupee sinks to 90.00/$ for first time.Japanese yen the main G10 FX decliner, Brazil’s real +0.5%.Bitcoin +6%. * BONDS: Long-dated JGB yields hit new highs beforecooling. Front-end U.S. yields slip on Trump’s Fed Chair remark,curve bull steepens. * COMMODITIES/METALS: Oil falls more than 1%. Gold hasbiggest fall in 2 weeks, but silver hugs record highs.

Today’s Talking Points

* Could Fed back away from rate cut?

When the Fed entered the ’blackout period’ last week ahead of its Dec 9-10 policy meeting, rates markets were pricing a near 100% chance of a rate cut. That fell to as low as 80% after manufacturing ISM data showed prices rising in October even though activity contracted again.

An 80% probability is still overwhelmingly strong odds for a cut. But what if Friday’s PCE inflation is higher than expected? Will traders reduce their rate cut bets further? The Fed would be loath to surprise markets, so Chair Powell will no doubt be hoping the PCE report is broadly in line with forecasts.

* Yield curves steepen as inflation percolates

If global signals are worth anything, the U.S. PCE inflation report on Friday may indeed offer an upside surprise. Last week figures showed Tokyo inflation slightly stronger than expected, supporting BOJ rate hike bets; on Tuesday, flash euro zone inflation estimates were also slightly higher than forecast, cooling any outside bets on ECB easing next year.

From a bond market perspective, the upshot is long yields creeping higher and curves steepening. Long-dated JGB yields are at record highs and the 2s/10s curve is the steepest since 2012; benchmark U.S. and euro zone curves are not far from their steepest levels since 2022-23.

* U.S. tech finds its Mojo

Although Wall Street hasn’t revisited its peaks from late October, AI sentiment is driving a wider recovery that has delivered six ’up’ days in the last seven. Tech is up around 8% in that time, and the Philadelphia Fed semiconductor index’s gains are double that.

The latest ’Big Tech’ tie-up will see Amazon’s AWS cloud computing unit adopt key Nvidia technology in future generations of AI computing chips, while Amazon also introduced new versions of its AI models known as Nova. Announcements like these are becoming more frequent, and sometimes they are treated with caution. Not today.

Global demand for US stocks isn’t waning. It’s increasing

Given the outperformance of many major European and Asian equity markets over Wall Street this year, it might appear that foreign investors are turning sour on U.S. equities.

But that’s not the case. Not only are overseas private sector inflows into U.S. stocks running at record levels, they have re-accelerated in recent months. The big question now is whether this can be sustained next year.

The latest official Treasury International Capital data - which comes with a lag but is the gold standard for measuring overseas appetite for U.S. assets - shows that net purchases of U.S. stocks from foreign private sector investors in the 12 months through September totaled $646.7 billion.

Indeed, TIC data shows that inflows from abroad have been breaking records by this measure almost every month this year, having smashed through the previous peak of $392 billion, from 2021, in January.

RE-ACCELERATING INFLOWS

That rolling 12-month figure is partially boosted by strong inflows around the 2024 U.S. Presidential election. Monthly net purchases of U.S. stocks from foreign private sector investors rarely exceed $100 billion, but they did in September and November last year as investors hoovered up stocks in the belief that an incoming Trump administration would pursue an unabashedly pro-growth, "market-friendly" agenda of slashing taxes and regulation.

That initial wave of bullish optimism faded early this year, however, and was quickly replaced by unease surrounding Trump’s tariffs and protectionist trade policy.

But overseas demand for U.S. stocks didn’t cool for long before the artificial intelligence frenzy brought it roaring back. Net foreign purchases have topped $100 billion in three of the past five months, and exceeded $90 billion in another.

The near $650 billion that overseas investors poured into Wall Street on a net basis in the year to September is around 40% of the net $1.59 trillion that flooded into U.S. assets in that time. It is the biggest foreign flow into any single U.S. asset class over the period - Treasuries drew in $493 billion, corporate bonds $319 billion, and agency debt $127.5 billion.

WEAK DOLLAR BOOSTS NON-U.S. RETURNS

In some ways, these inflows from abroad are unsurprising. The whole world has wanted in on the U.S.-led AI boom this year.

Yet other slices of capital flows paint a different picture, and many key stock markets around the world have matched or bettered Wall Street this year, especially since the post-"Liberation Day" lows in early April.

The S&P 500 may be up 15% in 2025 thus far, but the MSCI Asia ex-Japan index has increased nearly 25%, while both Germany’s DAX and Britain’s FTSE 100 are up almost 20%.

Importantly, gains in non-U.S. markets are in local currency, meaning in dollar terms they can be boosted even further thanks to the greenback’s decline. Brazil’s Bovespa is up 30% year-to-date, and up a further 20 percentage points in dollar terms.

All told, analysts at JP Morgan Asset Management estimated that international equities were outperforming their U.S. counterparts by 1,520 basis points in the year through mid-November, the biggest outperformance since 1993.

They estimate that the dollar is still 10% too expensive relative to "fair value" and reckon the U.S. equity premium over international stocks is 34%, substantially higher than the long-run average of 19%.

What’s more, the U.S. share of global equity market cap has risen to as much as 65% by some measures. So foreign allocation to U.S. stocks is still extreme, even if more of the dollar exposure is now hedged.

CAN IT CONTINUE?

Where non-U.S. investors put their next marginal dollar could depend largely on the answers to three market-specific questions for 2026: Are U.S. stocks too expensive? Can U.S. earnings remain so robust? And is AI a bubble?

There are three months of 2025 TIC flows still to be released, and it remains to be seen how investors’ sentiment has been affected by year-end profit-taking or the record-long U.S. government shutdown.

It’s plausible that the all-too familiar fears over U.S. valuations, market concentration, and future returns on AI spending could force overseas investors to slow their purchases of U.S. equities. Wall Street would be vulnerable to outright correction, relative underperformance, or both.

But the available evidence shows that, despite all the U.S. economic, political and policy turbulence in 2025, foreign appetite for U.S. stocks has never been stronger.

What could move markets tomorrow? * Australia GDP (Q3, final) * South Korea GDP (Q3, revised) * UK services PMI (November) * Euro zone producer inflation (October) * ECB chief economist Philip Lane speaks * U.S. ISM services (November) * U.S. ADP private sector employment (November) * U.S. industrial production (September)

Opinions expressed are those of the author. They do not reflect the views of Reuters News, which, under the Trust Principles, is committed to integrity, independence, and freedom from bias.

(By Jamie McGeever;)

title: Varonis integrates data security platform with AWS Security Hub
content:
Varonis Systems Inc. (VRNS) announced an integration with AWS Security Hub that combines automated threat detection and remediation capabilities across cloud environments.

The integration allows Varonis to ingest prioritized findings from AWS Security Hub and add context on data sensitivity, identity patterns, and user behavior to provide security teams with a consolidated view of risk across data sources and applications.

"By combining powerful insights and critical context on data and how it’s used, customers can quickly understand where data is at risk, remediate exposure and misconfigurations, and keep sensitive data safer," said David Bass, executive vice president of engineering and chief technology officer at Varonis.

The integration includes automated remediation features that can block public access to AWS Simple Storage Service buckets, remove inactive users and roles, and resolve misconfigurations such as missing password policies and encryption settings.

Varonis provides data discovery and classification, identity protection, and threat detection analytics for organizations using software-as-a-service, infrastructure-as-a-service, and hybrid cloud environments.

The announcement was made as part of the company’s expansion of automated remediation capabilities for AWS services, according to the press release statement.

title: Record shoppers, online sales surge signal strong US holiday season momentum
content:
Dec 2 (Reuters) - U.S. shoppers spent $14.25 billion on Cyber Monday, pushing total online sales over the Thanksgiving weekend to $44.2 billion, according to an Adobe Analytics report, as consumers lapped up offers on everything from gadgets to household essentials.

Spending rose 7.7% during the so-called Cyber Week - the five days from Thanksgiving to Cyber Monday - compared with an 8.2% increase to $41.1 billion last year and above its prior expectations of $43.7 billion, the report said on Tuesday.

A record 202.9 million U.S. customers shopped during the period, compared with the 197 million recorded last year, according to the National Retail Federation.

"This year’s record turnout reflects a highly engaged consumer who is focused on value, responds to compelling promotions, and seizes upon the opportunity to make the winter holidays special and meaningful," said NRF CEO Matthew Shay.

A total of 129.5 million consumers shopped in-store for the holidays, up 3% from a year earlier, the NRF said.

Shoppers spent $337.86 on average on items such as gifts, holiday apparel, decorations and other seasonal purchases, up from $315.56 in 2024, according to the NRF report.

Retailers including Amazon.com, Walmart and Target rolled out attractive discounts across categories to draw both affluent shoppers eager to splurge and budget-conscious consumers looking to stretch every dollar.

Even as big discounts led a few customers to take on short-term debt, analysts have noted that consumers remained savvy and watched price tags carefully to avoid impulse buys this year.

Several Americans relied on AI-powered services such as chatbots to browse products and compare prices in categories like appliances, toys, video games as well as jewelry.

title: AWS launches AI Factories for on-premises AI infrastructure
content:
LAS VEGAS - Amazon Web Services (AWS) announced AWS AI Factories at its re:Invent conference, offering dedicated AI infrastructure that can be deployed within customers’ existing data centers. This strategic move comes from Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN), which boasts a substantial market capitalization of $2.52 trillion and has achieved 11.48% revenue growth over the last twelve months.

The new offering combines NVIDIA accelerated computing platforms, AWS Trainium chips, AWS AI services, and high-speed networking in a managed package that allows organizations to maintain data within their facilities while leveraging AWS technology.

According to the company’s press release, AWS handles the deployment and management of the integrated infrastructure, allowing customers to utilize their existing data center space, network connectivity, and power resources.

The solution aims to help enterprises and public sector organizations address data sovereignty and regulatory requirements while accelerating their AI deployment timelines.

AWS AI Factories represents an extension of the company’s AI capabilities beyond its cloud infrastructure, potentially enabling organizations with strict data residency requirements to develop and deploy AI applications at scale.

Amazon Web Services, a subsidiary of Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN), made the announcement as part of its annual re:Invent conference taking place in Las Vegas.

In other recent news, Amazon Web Services (AWS) unveiled three new frontier agents at its AWS re:Invent conference. These include the Kiro autonomous agent, AWS Security Agent, and AWS DevOps Agent, each designed to operate independently in software development and security tasks. This development highlights AWS’s focus on enhancing its AI capabilities. Additionally, Sony Group Corporation is collaborating with AWS to use its AI services for creating the Sony Engagement Platform, aiming to foster deeper emotional connections with fans across its various entertainment sectors.

On the financial front, Amazon.com has seen several adjustments in stock price targets by major firms. Wells Fargo raised its target to $295, citing Amazon’s plans to double AWS capacity by 2027, which they believe could lead to revenue growth. Oppenheimer also increased its target to $305, maintaining an Outperform rating, and noted the potential upside due to AWS’s capacity expansion plans. Meanwhile, Citizens maintained a $300 price target, expressing some skepticism about Amazon’s ability to profitably scale its grocery business despite previous efforts to improve margins. These recent developments underscore the ongoing interest and analysis of Amazon’s growth strategies and market performance.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: AWS unveils three frontier agents to transform software development
content:
The three new offerings include Kiro autonomous agent, AWS Security Agent, and AWS DevOps Agent. According to the press release statement, Kiro functions as a virtual developer that maintains context and learns over time while working independently. The AWS Security Agent serves as a virtual security engineer to help build secure applications through consulting on app design, code reviews, and penetration testing. The AWS DevOps Agent is designed to help resolve and prevent incidents while improving application reliability and performance.

AWS characterized these frontier agents as a new class of AI tools that can operate autonomously for hours or days, allowing developers to focus on higher-priority tasks.

The announcement comes as AWS continues to expand its artificial intelligence offerings across its cloud computing platform. The company has been integrating AI capabilities throughout its services as part of its strategy to maintain its position in the cloud market.

AWS, which has been operating for nearly two decades, serves millions of customers across various industries and organization sizes. The company’s cloud infrastructure supports both traditional cloud computing and newer generative AI applications.

The frontier agents are the latest addition to AWS’s growing portfolio of AI-powered tools aimed at helping customers automate and enhance their software development and operations processes.

In other recent news, Amazon.com has seen several updates from analysts regarding its stock price targets. Oppenheimer raised its price target for Amazon to $305, citing potential growth in Amazon Web Services (AWS) capacity, which is expected to double by 2027. Similarly, Wells Fargo increased its price target to $295, emphasizing AWS’s anticipated revenue acceleration. Citizens maintained its $300 price target but expressed concerns about Amazon’s ability to profitably scale its grocery business despite previous efforts to improve margins. TD Cowen reiterated a Buy rating with a $300 price target, noting AWS’s accelerating revenue growth of 20.2% year-over-year in the third quarter and a significant backlog increase. Additionally, Sony Group Corporation is collaborating with Amazon Web Services to enhance its AI capabilities, aiming to improve fan engagement across its various business sectors. This partnership highlights Amazon’s ongoing influence and expansion in the technology and cloud services sectors. These developments reflect Amazon’s strategic focus on expanding its cloud services and addressing challenges in other business areas.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: Amazon launches four new Nova 2 AI models and training services
content:
Amazon Web Services announced the expansion of its Nova artificial intelligence portfolio with four new models, a custom model training service, and browser automation technology.

The Nova 2 models are designed for reasoning, multimodal processing, conversational AI, code generation, and automated tasks. Amazon stated the models deliver competitive price-performance metrics across these applications.

The company introduced Nova Forge, a service that allows organizations to build customized variants of Nova models by incorporating their proprietary data during the training process. Amazon describes this as an "open training" approach that enables companies to create optimized versions of the base models.

Amazon also unveiled Nova Act, a browser-based automation service. According to the company, early customers achieved 90% reliability rates for user interface automation workflows using the service.

The announcement was made during Amazon’s annual cloud computing conference in Las Vegas. The new offerings expand Amazon’s artificial intelligence capabilities as the company competes with other cloud providers in the growing AI services market.

Amazon Web Services has positioned itself as a provider of AI infrastructure and services to businesses of various sizes. The Nova portfolio represents the company’s effort to offer both pre-built AI models and customization options for enterprise customers.

The information was provided in a company press release statement.

title: AWS launches Trainium3 UltraServers with new 3nm AI chip
content:
Amazon Web Services announced the general availability of Trainium3 UltraServers at AWS re:Invent, featuring the company’s new Trainium3 chip built on 3-nanometer technology.

The Trainium3 UltraServers deliver up to 4.4 times more compute performance, four times greater energy efficiency, and nearly four times more memory bandwidth compared to previous-generation Trainium2 UltraServers, according to the company. The systems can scale up to 144 Trainium3 chips and provide up to 362 FP8 petaFLOPs with four times lower latency than the prior generation.

Several companies are already using the technology. Anthropic, Karakuri, Metagenomi, NetoAI, Ricoh, and Splash Music report reducing training and inference costs by up to 50% with Trainium systems. Decart achieved four times faster inference for real-time generative video at half the cost of graphics processing units, while Amazon Bedrock is serving production workloads on Trainium3.

The announcement was made at the AWS re:Invent conference in Las Vegas. Amazon Web Services is a subsidiary of Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN).

The new chip represents AWS’s continued investment in custom silicon designed specifically for artificial intelligence workloads, competing with established GPU providers in the AI training and inference market.

title: AWS launches three AI agents for software development teams
content:
Amazon Web Services announced three AI agents at its re:Invent conference: Kiro autonomous agent, AWS Security Agent, and AWS DevOps Agent. The company (AMZN) described these as "frontier agents" designed to work autonomously for extended periods without constant supervision.

Kiro autonomous agent functions as a virtual developer that maintains context and learns over time while working independently. The AWS Security Agent serves as a virtual security engineer, providing security consultation for application design, code reviews, and penetration testing. The AWS DevOps Agent acts as a virtual operations team member, helping resolve incidents and working to prevent future issues while improving application reliability and performance.

According to the press release, these agents represent a new category of AI tools that are autonomous and scalable, capable of operating for hours or days without intervention. The announcement was made during AWS re:Invent in Las Vegas.

AWS positioned these agents as extensions to software development teams, allowing developers to focus on higher-priority tasks while the AI agents handle routine operations, security assessments, and maintenance activities.

title: AWS launches AI factories for customer data centers
content:
Amazon Web Services announced AWS AI Factories at the AWS re:Invent conference in Las Vegas. The service provides dedicated infrastructure deployed in customers’ existing data centers to support AI application development and deployment.

AWS AI Factories combine NVIDIA accelerated computing platforms, Trainium chips, AWS AI services, and high-speed networking infrastructure. Customers can utilize their current data center space, network connectivity, and power systems while AWS manages the deployment and operation of the integrated infrastructure.

The service targets enterprises and public sector organizations seeking to meet data sovereignty and regulatory requirements. AWS (AMZN) handles the complexity of infrastructure management while customers maintain control over their existing facilities.

The announcement was made during AWS re:Invent, the company’s annual cloud computing conference. AWS AI Factories represent the company’s approach to bringing cloud-based AI capabilities directly to customer premises rather than requiring organizations to migrate workloads to AWS data centers.

The infrastructure includes AWS’s Trainium chips, which are custom-designed processors for machine learning training workloads. The system also incorporates NVIDIA’s accelerated computing technology alongside AWS’s networking and AI service offerings.

Organizations can accelerate their AI deployment timelines while maintaining compliance with data residency requirements through the on-premises infrastructure model. The service allows customers to leverage AWS’s AI capabilities without moving sensitive data off-site.

title: AWS unveils new Amazon Bedrock AgentCore capabilities at re:Invent
content:
Amazon Web Services announced enhancements to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore at the AWS re:Invent conference in Las Vegas. AWS is a subsidiary of Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN).

The new features include Policy functionality that blocks unauthorized agent actions through real-time controls operating outside agent code. AgentCore Evaluations allows developers to continuously assess agent quality based on behavior patterns. AgentCore Memory introduces episodic functionality designed to help agents learn from experiences.

Companies using AgentCore include Amazon Devices Operations & Supply Chain, Archera, Cohere Health, Cox Automotive, Druva, Heroku, Natera, NTT DATA, MongoDB, PGA TOUR, Pulumi, Thomson Reuters, Workday, Snorkel, Swisscom, and S&P Global Market Intelligence, according to the company’s statement.

The enhancements are part of AWS’s broader artificial intelligence services portfolio. Amazon Bedrock AgentCore is designed to help organizations build and deploy AI agents for production environments.

AWS re:Invent is the company’s annual cloud computing conference where it typically announces new services and updates to existing offerings. The information is based on an AWS press release.

title: Amazon enhances Bedrock AgentCore with new policy controls and evaluations
content:
Amazon Web Services announced enhancements to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, introducing policy controls, evaluation tools, and enhanced memory capabilities for AI agent development. The updates were revealed at the company’s re:Invent conference.

The new Policy feature allows organizations to set boundaries for agent actions using natural language commands. Teams can define which tools and data agents can access and under what conditions. The system operates through AgentCore Gateway to check agent actions against policies in milliseconds.

"Block all refunds from customers when the reimbursement amount is greater than $1,000" represents an example of how policies can be implemented, according to the announcement.

AgentCore Evaluations introduces 13 pre-built evaluators for quality dimensions including correctness, helpfulness, tool selection accuracy, safety, goal success rate, and context relevance. The service continuously samples live agent interactions and can trigger alerts when performance metrics decline.

The enhanced memory capability, called AgentCore Memory, enables agents to learn from past experiences through episodic functionality. This allows agents to build understanding of users over time and apply insights to future interactions without resetting context windows.

Several companies are implementing these capabilities. Druva, a data security provider, plans to use Policy controls for agent access to backup systems and security logs. Natera, a genetic testing company, will utilize AgentCore Evaluations to monitor AI agents used in oncology patient care while meeting healthcare compliance standards.

S&P Global Market Intelligence reported that AgentCore reduced agent deployment time from weeks to minutes for their internal Astra platform, which manages multi-agent workflows.

The announcement included a list of organizations using AgentCore, spanning companies from Amazon’s own operations to enterprises like Cox Automotive, MongoDB, PGA TOUR, Thomson Reuters, and Workday.

title: AWS launches AI factories for on-premises AI infrastructure deployment
content:
Amazon Web Services announced AWS AI Factories, a service that deploys dedicated AI infrastructure in customers’ data centers. The offering combines NVIDIA accelerated computing platforms, AWS Trainium chips, AI services, and high-speed networking infrastructure.

AWS AI Factories operate as private AWS regions within customer facilities, providing access to compute, storage, database, and AI services including Amazon Bedrock and SageMaker AI. The service allows organizations to use existing data center space, network connectivity, and power while AWS manages deployment and infrastructure operations.

The service targets enterprises and public sector organizations with data sovereignty and regulatory compliance requirements. AWS handles the complexity of AI infrastructure deployment, which the company states can reduce multi-year buildout timelines that organizations typically face when building their own AI capabilities.

AWS and NVIDIA expanded their collaboration for the AI Factories offering. The integration includes NVIDIA’s Grace Blackwell and Vera Rubin platforms with AWS Nitro System and Elastic Fabric Adapter networking. AWS plans to support NVIDIA NVLink Fusion technology in future Trainium4 and Graviton chips.

"Large-scale AI requires a full-stack approach—from advanced GPUs and networking to software and services that optimize every layer of the data center," said Ian Buck, vice president and general manager of Hyperscale and HPC at NVIDIA.

AWS announced a partnership with HUMAIN to build an "AI Zone" in Saudi Arabia featuring up to 150,000 AI chips, including GB300 GPUs, within a HUMAIN data center. The infrastructure will serve local and global AI compute demand.

AWS AI Factories are designed to meet security standards for government workloads across classification levels from Unclassified to Top Secret, according to the company’s statement.

title: AWS introduces three AI agents for software development tasks
content:
Amazon Web Services announced three AI agents designed to handle software development, security, and operations tasks with minimal human intervention. The Kiro autonomous agent, AWS Security Agent, and AWS DevOps Agent can operate for hours or days independently while completing complex projects.

The Kiro autonomous agent manages software development tasks across multiple repositories, maintaining context between sessions and learning from pull requests and feedback. It handles bug triaging, code coverage improvements, and multi-repository changes while integrating with GitHub, Jira, and Slack.

AWS Security Agent provides security guidance throughout the development process, reviewing design documents and scanning pull requests against organizational security requirements. The agent can conduct penetration testing and provide remediation code for identified vulnerabilities across AWS, multicloud, and hybrid environments.

AWS DevOps Agent focuses on operational tasks, responding to incidents and identifying root causes by analyzing application resources and telemetry data. Amazon reports the agent achieved over 86% accuracy in root cause identification after handling thousands of escalations within the company.

SmugMug, a photography platform, implemented AWS Security Agent to enhance its automated security testing. "AWS Security Agent helped catch a business logic bug that no existing tools would have caught," said Andres Ruiz, staff software engineer at SmugMug.

Commonwealth Bank of Australia tested AWS DevOps Agent on complex network and identity management issues. The agent identified root causes in under 15 minutes for problems that typically require hours of investigation by experienced engineers.

The agents integrate with existing development tools and can scale to handle multiple simultaneous tasks. AWS characterized these tools as "frontier agents" that represent autonomous, scalable AI systems capable of extended independent operation.

title: Amazon launches Nova 2 AI models and agent-building services
content:
Amazon Web Services announced the release of four new Nova 2 artificial intelligence models and two new services for organizations to build custom AI solutions, according to a company press release.

The Nova 2 model family includes Nova 2 Lite for everyday workloads, Nova 2 Pro for complex reasoning tasks, Nova 2 Sonic for speech-to-speech conversations, and Nova 2 Omni for multimodal processing. Nova 2 Omni can process text, images, video, and speech while generating both text and images.

AWS introduced Nova Forge, a service that allows organizations to create customized AI models by combining their proprietary data with Amazon’s Nova capabilities through what the company calls "open training." Customers can access pre-trained, mid-trained, and post-trained Nova model checkpoints to integrate their data at various training stages.

The company also launched Nova Act, a service for building AI agents that automate browser-based tasks. Amazon states Nova Act achieves 90% reliability for UI automation workflows among early customers. The service uses a custom Nova 2 Lite model trained through reinforcement learning on simulated web environments.

Organizations including Cisco, Siemens, Booking.com, Reddit, and 1Password are using the Nova services for various applications. Chris Slowe, CTO of Reddit, said the company is using Nova Forge to improve content moderation with a unified system that replaces multiple specialized models.

Companies can access Nova 2 Lite through Nova Forge immediately, with early access to Nova 2 Pro and Nova 2 Omni available for Nova Forge customers. The models are available through Amazon Bedrock, the company’s AI service platform.

AWS announced these developments as part of its re:Invent conference. The services are designed to compete with AI offerings from OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic in the enterprise market.

title: Sony taps Amazon’s AI services to boost fan engagement
content:
The collaboration aims to help Sony deliver "Kando," a Japanese concept for deep emotional connection, across its diverse business portfolio spanning electronics, gaming, music, movies, and anime.

Sony’s internal enterprise AI platform, powered by Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, currently processes 150,000 inference requests daily and is projected to grow 300-fold in the coming years. The platform helps employees with content drafting, inquiry responses, forecasting, fraud detection, and idea development.

The company is also utilizing the Amazon Nova Forge program to develop AI that could potentially increase efficiency in review and assessment processes by 100 times, based on early results.

Central to Sony’s business strategy is the Sony Engagement Platform, which includes Sony Data Ocean, a comprehensive data platform processing up to 760 terabytes of data from over 500 types of datasets across Sony Group companies. Built on AWS services including Amazon SageMaker, it provides AI-enhanced insights to connect fans with similar interests. Amazon’s cloud services division continues to drive the company’s impressive 11.48% revenue growth, contributing significantly to its $691.33 billion in trailing twelve-month revenue.

The platform extends core functions of PlayStation’s online infrastructure, such as accounts, payments, data capabilities, and security, to streamline operations and customer experience across various entertainment categories.

Tsuyoshi Kodera, chief digital officer at Sony Group Corporation, said the initiative represents "a significant step toward shaping the future of the entertainment industry."

Sony Group Corporation (NASDAQ:AMZN) operates across five business segments: Game & Network Services; Music; Pictures; Entertainment, Technology & Services; and Imaging & Sensing Solutions.

In other recent news, Amazon’s financial outlook has garnered attention from several analyst firms. TD Cowen reiterated a Buy rating with a $300 price target, highlighting Amazon Web Services’ (AWS) revenue acceleration, which reached 20.2% year-over-year growth in the third quarter of 2025. The firm noted a $200 billion backlog, showcasing a 22% increase from the previous year. Oppenheimer raised its price target to $305, citing AWS’s plans to double its capacity by 2027, having already doubled since 2022. Wells Fargo also adjusted its price target, increasing it to $295, and maintained an Overweight rating, supported by Amazon’s management expectations for AWS growth. Meanwhile, Citizens maintained a Market Outperform rating with a $300 price target but expressed uncertainty about the profitability of Amazon’s grocery business. BMO Capital reaffirmed its Outperform rating with a $300 price target, noting a potential slowdown in holiday sales growth based on their consumer survey. These developments reflect diverse analyst perspectives on Amazon’s future performance.

This article was generated with the support of AI and reviewed by an editor. For more information see our T&C.

title: NVIDIA and AWS expand AI infrastructure partnership with NVLink Fusion
content:
NVIDIA (NVDA) and Amazon Web Services announced an expanded strategic collaboration at AWS re:Invent, introducing new technology integrations across interconnect technology, cloud infrastructure and AI models.

AWS will support NVIDIA NVLink Fusion, a platform for custom AI infrastructure, to deploy its custom-designed silicon including next-generation Trainium4 chips for inference and agentic AI model training, Graviton CPUs and the Nitro System virtualization infrastructure.

The collaboration will combine NVIDIA NVLink scale-up interconnect and the NVIDIA MGX rack architecture with AWS custom silicon. AWS is designing Trainium4 to integrate with NVLink and NVIDIA MGX as part of a multigenerational collaboration between the companies.

AWS has expanded its accelerated computing portfolio with the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture, including NVIDIA HGX B300 and NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 GPUs. NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs for visual applications are expected to be available on AWS in the coming weeks.

The companies announced AWS AI Factories, a new AI cloud offering that will provide customers with dedicated infrastructure in their own data centers, operated by AWS, while allowing customers to maintain data control and comply with local regulations.

NVIDIA Nemotron open models are now integrated with Amazon Bedrock, enabling customers to build generative AI applications. Developers can access Nemotron Nano 2 and Nemotron Nano 2 VL models through the platform.

Amazon OpenSearch Service now offers serverless GPU acceleration for vector index building, powered by NVIDIA cuVS. AWS stated that early adopters have seen up to 10x faster vector indexing at 25% of the cost.

NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models are available as NVIDIA NIM microservices on Amazon EKS for real-time robotics control and simulation workloads, and on AWS Batch for batch-based tasks.

NVIDIA received the AWS Global GenAI Infrastructure and Data Partner of the Year award, recognizing technology partners with the Generative AI Competency.

title: Sony deploys AWS AI services for enterprise platform and fan engagement
content:
Sony Group Corporation announced it is using Amazon Web Services (AMZN) artificial intelligence services to build enterprise AI capabilities and create a fan engagement platform across its business segments including electronics, gaming, music, movies, and anime.

The company deployed Amazon Bedrock AgentCore to develop an internal enterprise AI platform that provides 57,000 employees access to generative AI and agentic AI services. The platform currently processes 150,000 inference requests daily and is projected to scale 300-fold within a few years, according to the press release.

Sony is participating in Amazon’s Nova Forge program to develop AI models for business operations. Early results indicate the AI model could increase efficiency of the company’s review and assessment processes by 100 times, the company stated.

The Sony Engagement Platform represents a central component of the company’s business strategy. The platform incorporates Sony Data Ocean, which processes up to 760 terabytes of data from over 500 dataset types across Sony Group companies using AWS services including Amazon SageMaker.

The engagement platform extends core functions from PlayStation’s online service infrastructure, including accounts, payments, data capabilities, and security features, to connect fans and content creators across entertainment categories.

"Through our long-standing partnership with AWS, we are now able to fully harness the power of data and AI to forge new bonds between fans and creators," said Tsuyoshi Kodera, chief digital officer of Sony Group Corporation.

AWS CEO Matt Garman said the partnership has evolved from powering gaming experiences to helping Sony deliver emotional connections at scale through enterprise AI transformation.

The announcement was made at AWS re:Invent conference. Sony operates across five business segments: Game & Network Services, Music, Pictures, Entertainment Technology & Services, and Imaging & Sensing Solutions.

title: Sun Country Airlines opens new base at CVG airport in early 2026
content:
Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) will establish a new operational base at Cincinnati/Northern Kentucky International Airport in early 2026. The base will officially open on January 31, 2026, according to a company statement.

The Minneapolis-based airline selected CVG to support its cargo operations and future passenger service expansion. The airport serves as a major Amazon air hub and sorting facility, positioning it as one of the fastest-growing cargo airports in the United States.

The operational base will function as a primary location where cargo aircraft are stationed and local crews begin and end flight assignments. Sun Country will utilize shared CVG facilities for its operations.

"Growing our footprint beyond Minneapolis-St. Paul has long been a goal for Sun Country, and we are excited to mark this major milestone," said Jude Bricker, president and chief executive officer. "Amazon and our cargo operation is a critical segment of our differentiated business model and enables our scheduled service seasonal flexibility and growth."

The airline is actively recruiting pilots for the CVG location. Sun Country indicated it is evaluating additional base locations to support both its Amazon cargo operations and scheduled service growth.

Sun Country operates as a hybrid low-cost carrier providing passenger service, charter flights, and cargo service to Amazon. The airline serves destinations throughout the United States and to locations in Mexico, Central America, Canada, and the Caribbean.

title: TEN Holdings partners with Webinar.net to accelerate platform development
content:
TEN Holdings Inc. (XHLD) announced a partnership with Webinar.net to accelerate development of its Ten Events Pro platform. The collaboration is expected to reduce the company’s operating expenses by $1.2 million to $1.7 million in 2026, according to a press release.

The partnership aims to triple the development speed of Ten Events Pro, bringing the platform to market ahead of its original timeline. TEN Holdings operates through its subsidiary Ten Events Inc., which provides event planning, production and broadcasting services.

"The partnership with Ten Events strengthens our position in the rapidly expanding corporate virtual events and webinars market," said Michael Nelson, President of Webinar.net. The collaboration provides TEN Holdings with access to cloud-based technology and enterprise-grade infrastructure.

The partnership includes immediate integrations with existing tools and enhanced artificial intelligence capabilities for the Ten Events Pro platform. TEN Holdings expects the accelerated development to support faster annual recurring revenue growth and improved market positioning.

"This is more than a product milestone; it’s a turning point for Ten Events," said Randolph Wilson Jones III, Chief Executive Officer of TEN Holdings. "The accelerated development of Ten Events Pro puts us years ahead of schedule and opens the door to new opportunities for long-term growth."

Webinar.net operates on Amazon Web Services infrastructure and provides virtual event technology to enterprise clients. TEN Holdings is headquartered in Pennsylvania and produces virtual, hybrid and physical events using its proprietary Xyvid Pro Platform.

title: Amazon cuts seller fees in Europe in Shein, Temu price war
content:
By Helen Reid

LONDON, Dec 2 (Reuters) - Amazon is cutting the fees it charges sellers in Europe as it responds to competition from marketplaces like Shein and Temu that sell clothes, homeware and gadgets at rock-bottom prices.

The move, which Amazon said in a statement on Tuesday was one of its largest ever fee reductions, is focused first on lowering fees for sellers of cheap fashion.

It cuts the referral fees Amazon charges on clothes and accessories to 5% from 7% for items up to 15 euros ($17.41) or 15 pounds ($19.79), and to 10% from 15% for items between 15 and 20 euros or pounds, effective from December 15.

Shein charges sellers a referral fee of 10% for its European Union sites and 12.24% for Britain, according to its website, with zero referral fees for new sellers for the first 30 days.

"As we continue to lower our cost to serve through operational improvements and innovation, we are passing on greater savings to our selling partners," Amazon said.

Shein, which sells tops for 3 euros and jeans for as little as 8.20 euros, has taken market share from European fast-fashion retailers and challenged Amazon with its marketplace selling everything from duvet covers to vacuum cleaners.

Amazon is the dominant ecommerce platform in Germany, France, and other European countries, in a market where revenue is expected to grow 7% to hit 900 billion euros this year, industry association Ecommerce Europe forecast.

Additionally, Amazon said that it would cut referral fees from February 1 for home products to 8% from 15% for items up to 20 euros or pounds, as well as cutting fees on pet clothing, grocery and vitamins.

It will also reduce fulfilment fees for parcels by an average of 0.32 euros or 0.26 pounds in its Germany, France, Italy, Spain, and British stores from December 15.

($1 = 0.8615 euros)

($1 = 0.7578 pounds)

title: U.S. set to move from containment to tech rivalry with China in 2026 - Jefferies
content:
Investing.com - U.S. policymakers are likely to focus on competing with China’s tech ambitions next year after having attempted the contain the sector in the Asian nation over the last decade, according to analysts at Jefferies.

In a note to clients, the strategists including Aniket Shah and Charles Boakye argued that Beijing has secured "dominance" across the tech industry. They added that the tech rivalry between the U.S. and China now constitutes "the most important question shaping geopolitics over the next few decades."

Against this backdrop, the analysts predicted that "2026 will mark a turning point in the U.S.’s posture -- from attempts of containment, to tech-competition across several fronts."

Washington could focus on industrial policy and look to coordinate with "key players including universities [and] national labs" on its response to China’s emergence as a tech powerhouse, they added.

Evidence of this drive was seen last week, when U.S. President Donald Trump established through an executive order a national initiative to accelerate scientific discovery through artificial intelligence, the analysts said. Under the plan, which is known as the Genesis Mission, the Department of Energy will lead the development of a new platform aimed at unifying resources across labs, public science, and private-industry partners like tech companies.

Research workflows in cutting-edge areas such as advanced materials, biotechnology, and quantum computing would be accelerated by the proposal, with the timelines for the discovery and commercialization of new technologies shortened.

For investors, Trump’s plan is largely focused on monetizing innovations, an objective that "could reshape capital expenditures and how/who long term growth accrues to," the Jefferies analysts said. They flagged that China has built a "structural advantage" in this area over the last ten years, powering ahead with advances in products like electric vehicles, solar panels and robotics.

Deals between national labs and mega-cap tech groups -- including AI-darling Nvidia, Google-parent Alphabet, e-commerce giant Amazon, software giant Microsoft, Instagram-owner Meta, and electric carmaker Tesla -- "should provide an indication of where private partners are willing to commit resources," the analysts said.

title: Amazon stock price target raised to $295 from $292 at Wells Fargo
content:
The firm cited Amazon’s management expectation to double AWS capacity by 2027, which could support multi-quarter revenue acceleration for the cloud business. This expansion aligns with Amazon’s strong revenue growth trajectory, which has reached 11.48% over the last twelve months with total revenue of $691.33 billion.

The analyst projects that Amazon’s plan to double capacity implies at least 12GW of additional compute by year-end 2027, requiring approximately $270 billion in capital expenditure.

The forecast includes a mix of 3.5GW CPU ($39 billion annualized revenue) with the remaining 8.5GW split between Trainium (4.25GW = $25 billion) and Nvidia GPUs (4.25GW = $86 billion), scaling AWS global capacity to 24GW by Q4 2027 from 12GW in Q3 2025.

In other recent news, Amazon’s financial outlook has been the focus of several analyst firms. Oppenheimer raised its price target for Amazon to $305, citing the significant potential for Amazon Web Services (AWS) to double its capacity through 2027. This follows AWS’s reported acceleration in revenue growth, which reached 20.2% year-over-year in the third quarter of 2025, as noted by TD Cowen. The firm also highlighted a $200 billion backlog in AWS, representing 22% growth from the previous year.
